title: Preening
content:
The sky is red and comes
        from Montreal - 
        you lied to me
        the hemlock of the wind
        is not this January's
        but is ringed with
        steel laughter of
        another winter.

        I saw you wringing sweat
        from the eyes of the road,
        lie down take the season's
        wetness in your mouth,
        push apart moist dampness
        'til one cavity was
        felled and another opened.
meaning:
Paul Cameron Brown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Preening"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
